commit.message:
Fix TCP proxy connectivity — segment large payloads, fix threading and retransmit handling

- Split server responses into 1460-byte TCP segments to respect tunnel MTU;
  oversized packets (e.g. TLS certificate chains) were silently dropped,
  causing Safari/Reddit to time out during TLS handshake
- Run NWConnection callbacks on sessionQueue (not .global) to eliminate data
  races on state, pendingFromClient, and sequence numbers
- Detect and ignore retransmitted client segments to prevent double-buffering
  corrupted TLS streams
- Handle retransmitted SYNs by resending SYN-ACK instead of creating a new session
- Add MSS=1460 option to SYN-ACK so device uses full-size segments
- Drop UDP/443 (QUIC) to force Safari to use TCP
- Add CLAUDE.md with architecture docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: enforce Lettuce command-level timeout to prevent Redis hang

spring.data.redis.timeout only sets socket-level timeout; Lettuce ignores it
during command execution when Redis is unresponsive. Add TimeoutOptions.enabled(250ms)
via ClientOptions so every Redis command reliably times out within 250ms.

Also add /actuator/health/liveness (ping-only) group for deploy health checks
to avoid DB pool wait blocking the health probe.

commit.message:
fix(bind): reset bot-to-bot counter on rebind to prevent stale rate limits

When an entity is rebound (new bot), its bot-to-bot message counter is now
reset to 0. This prevents accumulated counters from previous bindings from
blocking broadcasts/messages on the freshly bound entity.

Fixes intermittent test-broadcast.js 429 rate limit failures in regression suite.

commit.message:
docs: add README, AGENTS.md, alerts docs, update features plan (#26)

* feat: alerts system — SRS due, snooze expired, stale notes

Adds a computed alerts feed with 3 initial alert types:
- SRS due: permanent notes with overdue spaced repetition
- Snooze expired: inbox notes past their snooze reviewAt
- Stale notes: capture notes untouched for 7+ days

Alerts are derived from state (not stored), only dismissed IDs
are persisted. Includes /alerts page, sidebar nav with count badge,
store slice, and v19 migration.

commit.message:
feat: pre-generate question cache to eliminate Gemini wait time (#56)

An EventBridge rule fires every hour (08:00–20:00 UTC) and pre-generates
up to 5 questions per recently active user, stored in the Sessions table.

When the user types /next, the bot pops a question from the cache
instantly. A live Gemini call is only made on cache miss (rare).

- database_dynamo.py / database.py: get_question_cache / set_question_cache
- lambda_handler.py: _run_cache_refill, _refill_user_cache, _generate_one_cached
- bot.py: _send_question checks cache before calling Gemini
- template.yaml: QuestionCacheRefill EventBridge rule cron(0 8-20 * * ? *)

Co-authored-by: Esref Ozturk <esref@Ozges-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: replace hook script with clay check-generated command

Instead of generating a bash script that uses python3 to check the
.clay manifest, the Claude Code hook now calls `clay check-generated`
directly. This keeps all logic in the clay binary — update once,
all projects benefit.

init-claude now only creates .claude/settings.json pointing to the
clay command. No scripts or hooks directory needed.

docs: CHANGELOG v0.4.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Motion → OmniFocus field updates and fix data type conversion bugs

Reverse sync now propagates due date, defer/start date, duration, and
priority changes from Motion back to OmniFocus when Motion's timestamp
is newer (last-writer-wins). Previously only completions and new task
creation flowed Motion → OF.

Key fixes included in this change:
- Fix AppleScript date parsing: construct dates via explicit year/month/day
  properties instead of locale-dependent `date "YYYY-MM-DD"` coercion
- Fix stale cache: refresh_motion_tasks_from_api now updates existing task
  fields (dueDate, startOn, duration, priority, status, updatedTime,
  description) instead of only adding new tasks
- Filter sentinel dates: Motion placeholder values (2000-12-30 for startOn,
  2000-12-31 for dueDate) are now excluded from sync
- Normalize duration types: int() normalization prevents spurious updates
  from int(30) != "30" and filters "NONE"/"REMINDER" strings
- Support clearing fields: update_task emits `missing value` for None
  dates/durations instead of silently skipping
- Fix description churn: strip "Start date:" prefix from Motion description
  body before comparison to prevent spurious overwrites each cycle
- Fix timestamp comparison: use datetime.fromisoformat() instead of string
  comparison for Z vs +00:00 format consistency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: omnifocus_to_motion_sync
repository.description: This script bridges OmniFocus and Motion to keep your tasks aligned across platforms. It supports syncing folders, projects, and tasks with detailed conditions, handling task creation and deletion intelligently. You can run in dry_run mode to preview actions without changes, or fully synchronize in sync mode.

docs: Add comprehensive code audit report

Identified 20+ issues across the codebase with detailed analysis:

High Priority:
- Version mismatch across main.py, pyproject.toml, agent/__init__.py
- Security issue: shell=True in test_runner.py (command injection risk)
- State management bug in run_agent restart loop

Medium Priority:
- Token estimation inaccuracy (char/4 formula)
- Magic number 999 for default priority in task_selector
- Blocking input() in human_intervention.py
- ConfigWatcher blocks event loop
- Missing module exports in agent/__init__.py

Documentation:
- MEMORY.txt vs MEMORY.md inconsistency fixed in analysis
- Added suggested follow-up tasks to feature_list.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: initial SQS transport implementation

Symfony bundle providing AWS SQS transport for Freyr Message Broker:

- SqsOutboxPublisher: implements OutboxPublisherInterface, routes to SQS
  via Symfony Messenger sender, attaches AmazonSqsFifoStamp for FIFO queues
- Routing strategy: convention-based with YAML overrides (sender per message)
- FIFO support: auto-detect .fifo suffix + explicit config flag, maps
  PartitionKeyStamp to MessageGroupId and MessageIdStamp to MessageDeduplicationId
- Topology management: queue creation with attributes, DLQ redrive policies
- CLI command: message-broker:setup-sqs with --dry-run, --dump, live modes
- Bundle DI: configurable transport name for AMQP+SQS coexistence
- 39 tests, PHPStan level max, ECS clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Engine 8 (Resonance) + Engine 9 (Director) prior art declarations

Added two new engines to the prior art declaration (9 engines total):

Engine 8 — Resonance (Cognitive Coherence): peer-to-peer cognitive modulation
field, coherence-gated context injection, coherence-driven planning, coherence-
aware background autonomy.

Engine 9 — Director (Cognitive Multiplexer): cognitive-state-aware tool selection,
synthesized multi-signal meta-directive, cognitive vetoes (appropriateness-based),
mid-request stance recomputation, alien-to-background-agent pipeline, dream-time
cognitive calibration.

Updated cross-engine integration diagram, proof section, and prior art table.
